items.title: Walter lost his wife and daughters at Port Arthur. Thirty years on, this is his message

children.content: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

gallery.altText: a bald man in a light blue t shirt
A man with his two daughters in a forest setting.

thumbnail.altText: a bald man in a light blue t shirt
A man with his two daughters in a forest setting.

items.title: Ancient and 'sterile' plant population found, but its location remains a secret

children.content: A second population of an ancient tree, King's lomatia, has been found in the south-west of Tasmania. A scientist says the precise location must be kept secret, or risk being 'killed by love'.

image.altText: Woman looks at a small plant in a nursery

items.title: Anzac biscuit-tin guitars sold to aid veteran charity

children.content: A Tasmanian guitar maker will donate Anzac biscuit-tin guitars to his local RSL to raise money for veteran mental health charity.

image.altText: guitars made from biscuit tins and the man who makes them

items.title: The disaster that 'shocked' a town and brought worldwide attention

children.content: For a dramatic fortnight, the plight of two miners trapped deep underground in Tasmania's north captivated the world. Twenty years later, the Beaconsfield rescue continues to defy belief.

gallery.altText: LtoR Todd Russell and Brant Webb wave as they emerge from the mine lift

thumbnail.altText: LtoR Todd Russell and Brant Webb wave as they emerge from the mine lift

items.title: Inside Chernobyl is a toxic world where frogs have turned black

children.content: Nuclear meltdown in 1986 reshaped ecosystems and human lives. Four decades on the legacy of the Chernobyl disaster is still unfolding.

gallery.altText: Two brown eyes with yellow and black nuclear symbols painted around them
Metal cots all burnt and rusted lined up in a destroyed room with broken dolls lying on each one

thumbnail.altText: Two brown eyes with yellow and black nuclear symbols painted around them
Metal cots all burnt and rusted lined up in a destroyed room with broken dolls lying on each one

items.title: Social licence test shows why Labor acts on NDIS but not gas

children.content: Labor's radical transformation of the NDIS as it resists a growing chorus of support for a new gas tax exposes the limit of its willingness to act when given a social licence.

image.altText: The prime minister looks at journalists.

items.title: Sex workers speak out about being caught in the middle of PNG's HIV crisis

children.content: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

image.altText: An image consisting of three panels showing condoms in one, a de-identified human in the next and an HIV testing kit in the last

items.title: Footballer returns to field after 15 days in induced coma

children.content: A Gippsland footballer who spent 15 days in an induced coma after contracting a rare nerve condition has made a remarkable return to the field.

image.altText: A photo of a male football player in a white, blue, red and black singlet and blue football shorts, on a football field

items.title: In the aftermath of complex grief there can be post-traumatic growth

children.content: It's normal to feel distress when you go through trauma. But some people also experience personal growth to make positive changes in their lives.

image.altText: Image of Blake Johnston surfing a wave wearing a cowboy hat.

items.title: At 85, Ringo Starr is still chasing his country music dream

children.content: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

gallery.altText: Ringo Starr stands in an outdoor garden wearing sunglasses, t shirt and jacket. He holds up a peace sign to the camera
Ringo Starr and T Bone Burnett sit at a mixing desk in a wood panelled recording studio. They are both laughing.

thumbnail.altText: Ringo Starr stands in an outdoor garden wearing sunglasses, t shirt and jacket. He holds up a peace sign to the camera
Ringo Starr and T Bone Burnett sit at a mixing desk in a wood panelled recording studio. They are both laughing.

items.title: Ollie Hollands 'in a good space' despite late omission after brother's struggles

children.content: Carlton coach Michael Voss says Elijah Hollands's younger brother Ollie is doing OK despite being a late out for the club's Anzac Day clash with Fremantle.

image.altText: Ollie Hollands points while on the field at Carlton AFL training.

items.title: Two out of three stolen cars are left with 'some form of hazard' in them

children.content: 'Opportunistic' car thieves are leaving vehicles littered with syringes, weapons and even traps, and in 2025, 77 per cent of vehicles inspected by one contractor had signs of methylamphetamine used at levels harmful to health.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt sitting in front of a blue ute in his driveway.

items.title: If you recogised the typo, you'll nail our spelling quiz

children.content: Delighted by diagraphs or hyped for homophones? Step inside and showcase your spelling skills.

gallery.altText: The comedian Guy Montgomery, a man in his mid-30s in a 70s tan suit on the set of his Spelling Bee

thumbnail.altText: The comedian Guy Montgomery, a man in his mid-30s in a 70s tan suit on the set of his Spelling Bee

items.title: You can holiday for free at this remote island, but there's a catch

children.content: North West Island is one of five national parks where you can holiday in exchange for campground hosting duties.

image.altText: An aerial image of a green coral cay island surrounded by reef

items.title: Outback post office to stay open thanks to work of local women

children.content: Dirranbandi residents would have faced a two-hour car ride just to post a letter if it weren't for the work of these locals.

image.altText: Jane and Montana stand on the steps of the Rural Transaction Centre in Dirranbandi.

items.title: Russian attack on Ukrainian apartment block kills seven, injures dozens

children.content: An apartment block in the south-eastern city of Dnipro was struck twice by Russian drones, officials say.

gallery.altText: A young man stands with a foil blanket around him surrounded by rubble.

thumbnail.altText: A young man stands with a foil blanket around him surrounded by rubble.

items.title: Housing developer Assemble slashes number of promised affordable homes

children.content: Victorian planning authorities will allow Assemble to significantly reduce the number of affordable homes offered at large developments in Melbourne's north by more than half.

items.title: Can commercial farming and conservation co-exist?

children.content: A predator-proof fence at a Victorian farm is helping endangered species recover while proving conservation and agriculture can successfully co-exist.

image.altText: A grey coloured small eastern barred bandicoot.

items.title: 'Tombstone tourists' keep Australia's oldest surviving European cemetery alive

children.content: Nestled between apartment blocks and opposite an escape room, St John's Cemetery holds many unique stories from history.

gallery.altText: Ms Dunn wearing gloves and a hat holds a pair of garden shearers she is using to remove weeds from a grave.

thumbnail.altText: Ms Dunn wearing gloves and a hat holds a pair of garden shearers she is using to remove weeds from a grave.

items.title: Reed to keep starting role after Dragons suffer Anzac Day belting

children.content: Dean Young backs Kade Reed to remain St George Illawarra's halfback even after the Dragons slumped to a 62-16 thrashing in the 20-year-old's highly anticipated NRL debut.

image.altText: Kade Reed of the Dragons looks down with disappointment

items.title: Bitumen standards lowered after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

image.altText: A road in the sunset, the flinders rangers can be seen in the distance

items.title: Threatened fish rescued in Victoria's 'most flow-stressed river'

children.content: A small fish that smells like freshly-cut cucumber could be akin to a canary in the coalmine for one of Victoria's most stressed rivers.

items.title: Cox's goal celebration sparks scuffle as Freo holds off Carlton comeback

children.content: Mason Cox gets in the face of Ben Ainsworth after kicking a fourth-quarter goal, resulting in some fireworks as Fremantle wins its sixth consecutive match.

image.altText: Mason Cox scuffles with Ben Ainsworth

items.title: Captain Grant bristles at 'silly question' as Storm sink to historic low

children.content: Melbourne captain Harry Grant has defended coach Craig Bellamy after the Storm sank to a historic low following a 48-6 belting at the hands of South Sydney.

image.altText: Captain and coach at the press conference, sitting, answering questions

items.title: Black Ferns outclass Wallaroos in Anzac Day Test

children.content: The Black Ferns produce a dominant performance to defeat the Wallaroos 40-5 in the first Anzac Day Test between the trans-Tasman rivals.

image.altText: a Wallaroos player is tackled b y Black Ferns opponents.

items.title: AFL sacks appeals boss after homophobia statement labelled 'disgusting'

children.content: Will Houghton KC is sacked as the chair of the AFL appeals board after the body's widely criticised statement and decision in the case of St Kilda player Lance Collard.

image.altText: The AFL logo on the side of a building, partially obscured by a tree branch.

items.title: 'Truly exceptional person': Australian Olympian Gordon Ingate dies aged 100

children.content: The Australian Olympian was still competing and winning into his 90s.
